column12: ID=g6884.t1;Parent=g6884;em_target=48698.ENSPFOP00000004049;em_score=869.0;em_evalue=0.0;em_tcov=100.0;em_OGs=COG0343@1|root,KOG3908@2759|Eukaryota,38H9M@33154|Opisthokonta,3BB83@33208|Metazoa,3CT6A@33213|Bilateria,486UH@7711|Chordata,48Y30@7742|Vertebrata,49PZG@7898|Actinopterygii;em_COG_cat=A;em_desc=Catalytic subunit of the queuine tRNA-ribosyltransferase (TGT) that catalyzes the base-exchange of a guanine (G) residue with queuine (Q) at position 34 (anticodon wobble position) in tRNAs with GU(N) anticodons (tRNA-Asp, -Asn, -His and -Tyr), resulting in the hypermodified nucleoside queuosine (7-(((4,5-cis- dihydroxy-2-cyclopenten-1-yl)amino)methyl)-7-deazaguanosine). Catalysis occurs through a double-displacement mechanism. The nucleophile active site attacks the C1' of nucleotide 34 to detach the guanine base from the RNA, forming a covalent enzyme-RNA intermediate.
